flags.concern: Pointer capture cleanup: the plan never touches TimelineCanvas's resize teardown, but the current resize handles only end sessions on pointer up/cancel. There is no lost-pointer-capture fallback, so the reported 'doesn't release frames after release' symptom can still persist if capture is dropped mid-resize.
flags.evidence: Addressed both significant flags. FLAG-001: added a new Step 2 that wires `onLostPointerCapture` on TimelineCanvas resize handles, routes through `endResize()` + `notifyInteractionEndIfIdle()`, and adds a regression test — directly tied to the "doesn't release frames after release" user symptom. FLAG-002: resolved the acceptance-command ambiguity by specifying exact gate commands in the Overview (filtered `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` over `src/tools/video-editor/` lines, plus `vitest run src/tools/video-editor`) and reused them in Step 9. Renumbered downstream steps. Tightened `imageClipSnapshot` to store absolute bounds (resolves an open metadata question). No scope creep — all changes stay inside `src/tools/video-editor`.

flags.concern: Typecheck scope: the plan treats 'tsc --noEmit clean for video-editor tree' as an acceptance gate, but this repository does not define a video-editor-only TypeScript project or script. Without first specifying how to scope or filter TypeScript errors, that success criterion is not executable against the actual repo.
flags.evidence: Addressed both significant flags. FLAG-001: added a new Step 2 that wires `onLostPointerCapture` on TimelineCanvas resize handles, routes through `endResize()` + `notifyInteractionEndIfIdle()`, and adds a regression test — directly tied to the "doesn't release frames after release" user symptom. FLAG-002: resolved the acceptance-command ambiguity by specifying exact gate commands in the Overview (filtered `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` over `src/tools/video-editor/` lines, plus `vitest run src/tools/video-editor`) and reused them in Step 9. flags.concern: Legacy-field verification: the cleanup step's proposed grep for plain 'start' and 'children' across src/tools/video-editor will false-fail against legitimate derived shot-group and timeline code, so it does not actually verify that only PinnedShotGroup.start/PinnedShotGroup.children were removed.
flags.evidence: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:12-15 intentionally defines derived ShotGroup.start and ShotGroup.children, and src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:507-515 legitimately reads group.children/group.start from that derived ShotGroup shape. A raw grep for 'start' or 'children' across the tree will hit many non-legacy uses.
